title: Fermented Mexican Oyster Stir-Fry
description: This adventurous and chewy dish features fermented oysters in a low-fat-friendly, savory sauce and mixed with vegetables for a unique twist on traditional norteño cuisine.
instructions: Place oysters in a colander and rinse under cold water. Pat dry with paper towels.
Heat oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add bell peppers, onions, garlic, ginger, and soy sauce if using. Stir-fry for about 5 minutes until vegetables are softened but not mushy.
Toss in the fermented oysters and continue to stir-fry for another 3-4 minutes or until all ingredients are heated through and oysters become crispy on the outside.
Squeeze lime juice over the stir-fry mixture. Adjust seasoning with salt if needed, then serve hot.
